page: Microsoft Azure F32-16amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famdsv7 family. It is configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system features 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 3780 GB, and the server is premium storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU benchmark, gzip single-threaded compression, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression all rank in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded performance is also notable, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression placing in the top 25% tier. However, multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations on large uncached blocks, consistently register average performance. Small-model LLM inference text generation ranks in the top 10% tier, while medium and large model inference speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F32-16amds_v7 offers high resource density, combining substantial memory capacity and local storage on a 16-core footprint. This makes it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive multi-core scaling but benefit from high single-thread performance. Appropriate workloads include memory-intensive databases, caching layers such as Redis, data compression tasks, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F32-16amds_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Famdsv7 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high ratio of 16.0 GB per core, alongside 3780 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression ranking in the top 10% tier. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, caching systems, and data compression tasks that benefit from strong single-threaded execution and high memory density without requiring GPU acceleration.
